title: paeonia corallina subsp. atlantica
intro: Paeonia corallina subsp. atlantica is a subspecies of the perennial herb Paeonia corallina, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a member of the peony family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including menstrual cramps, anxiety, and insomnia, due to its ability to calm the nervous system and reduce inflammation. Paeonia corallina subsp. atlantica is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
